message:
fix(config): use yq strenv() + validate key path + surface persist errors

Three chained bugs in dsr config_set()'s persistence path:

1. `yq -i --arg v "$value" ".$key = \$v" "$DSR_CONFIG_FILE"` was
   written in jq style, but the Mike Farah yq (Go) that dsr ships
   against does NOT accept `--arg`. The flag was silently ignored,
   `\$v` was interpreted as the literal string "$v" by yq, and
   every `config_set foo bar --persist` persisted the string "$v"
   to the file instead of "bar". This had gone unnoticed because
   the in-memory DSR_CONFIG always held the correct value for the
   remainder of the session — only the persisted file was wrong.

2. `.$key` interpolates `$key` unquoted into the yq expression. A
   key containing quotes, whitespace, arithmetic operators, or
   other yq syntax would let the caller inject yq expressions.
   dsr's own config keys are dotted-identifier-only by convention,
   but the function is reachable from user-supplied CLI
   arguments. Add an explicit whitelist
   `^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*(\.[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)*$` and
   return exit 4 for anything outside it, with a clear
   "Refusing to persist key with unsafe characters: $key" error.

3. The yq invocation's exit status was discarded and
   `_cfg_log_ok "Set $key = $value (persisted)"` ran
   unconditionally. A permission error, missing yq binary, or
   malformed config file produced a green "persisted" log entry
   with no actual write. Wrap the yq call in `if … ; then …
   else _cfg_log_error … ; return 1 ; fi` so callers get a
   non-zero status and a diagnostic when persistence genuinely
   fails.

Fix: validate the key, pass the value through the environment as
DSR_SET_VALUE, and read it back inside the yq expression via
strenv(DSR_SET_VALUE) — the yq-native way to splice external
data without risking yq-syntax interpretation. Redirect stderr to
/dev/null so a yq error doesn't double-print alongside our
_cfg_log_error line.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -295,9 +295,25 @@ config_set() {
     DSR_CONFIG["$key"]="$value"
 
     if $persist && command -v yq &>/dev/null; then
-        # Use --arg to safely escape the value
-        yq -i --arg v "$value" ".$key = \$v" "$DSR_CONFIG_FILE"
-        _cfg_log_ok "Set $key = $value (persisted)"
+        # Only accept dotted-path keys built from identifier characters —
+        # anything else could inject yq expression syntax (e.g. quotes or
+        # arithmetic) through the unquoted interpolation below.
+        if [[ ! "$key" =~ ^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*(\.[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)*$ ]]; then
+            _cfg_log_error "Refusing to persist key with unsafe characters: $key"
+            return 4
+        fi
+
+        # yq (Mike Farah Go version) does NOT accept jq-style --arg flags;
+        # pass the value through the environment and read it back with
+        # strenv() inside the expression so the YAML string escaping is
+        # handled by yq itself. Check the exit code so we surface errors
+        # instead of claiming success when persistence actually failed.
+        if DSR_SET_VALUE="$value" yq -i ".$key = strenv(DSR_SET_VALUE)" "$DSR_CONFIG_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
+            _cfg_log_ok "Set $key = $value (persisted)"
+        else
+            _cfg_log_error "Failed to persist $key to $DSR_CONFIG_FILE"
+            return 1
+        fi
     else
         _cfg_log_info "Set $key = $value (in memory)"
     fi
